sprint: Sparkle Trail / Settle Bounce Contrast Fix
task: A direct, same-session follow-up to a second user report: "throttled sparkle trail is not visible. neither the soft bounce." Investigated via real pixel-level evidence (a zoomed screenshot at the exact moment sparkles were confirmed spawning with real non-zero computed opacity/transform) rather than trusting the mechanism alone -- confirming this wasn't just hard to notice, it was a genuine contrast/scale defect: a 12px pale-gold glyph at 20-75% opacity has almost no luminance difference against this app's own light cream/white palette, so it rendered essentially invisibly even though every mechanism underneath it fired correctly. Fix, in css/style.css: sparkle glyph size raised 12px->20px; a white halo layered under the existing gold glow so it reads against any background (light meadow, white panel, or the dark ceremony overlay); every keyframe's peak scale raised so the effective rendered size is genuinely eye-catching. The settle-bounce keyframe's amplitude widened (+-3px/+-2px translateY, scale .93-1.05 -> +-7px/+-5px, scale .87-1.1) and duration extended .42s->.5s for a clearer 'boing' on a ~92px object; js/companionEngine.js's SETTLE_CLASS_MS bumped 420->520 to match so the class outlives the animation. Verified via the same zoomed-screenshot technique that first caught the defect: post-fix, the sparkle is a clearly visible bright white-gold burst in a real screenshot, not just a passing computed-style assertion. Full regression across the existing Story Egg Interaction & Presence suite (unchanged -- none of it asserted exact keyframe pixel values) plus the Companion Canon/Magic Card/Creator Playwright suite and both goldenBuild.js suites, all passing. A stray, untracked, empty assets/companions/ directory (a leftover filesystem artifact from an earlier sprint's own folder relocation, invisible to git status since git never tracks empty directories) was found and removed during this verification pass. No Theme Contract/Engine V2 Scene Model/Builder change, no Companion Registry-mechanism/Package-Contract redesign; confined to css/style.css and one constant in js/companionEngine.js.
